reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: only positive is the fact in walking distance to Disneyland. Avoid rooms facing the highway, very very noisy....

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.negatives:
Staff is rude and inconsiderate! 
!

reviews.title: unfriendly staff. the receptionist didnt have heart
reviews.text: My group has 5 adult , 1 baby 8 months, 1 kid 8 years old. i have party with friends . I gave my passport to my sister for checking in for everyone. Weather is very cold with my baby. But the receptionist guy said my sister was not allowed to check in, because the booking is under my name, although i already gave her my passport. it is too strictly . My baby got jet lag & sick for 1 hour at front desk, in order to wait for my coming

reviews.title: We will not stay here again
reviews.positives: Location was a decent. The breakfast was standard hotel buffet food. The hotel was clean.
reviews.negatives: We had to call for housekeeping services every day we were there. We were forced to valet our car, and we were not allowed to access our vehicle unless valet staff was present. We had a Valet driver drove our vehicle recklessly when pulled it out of the underground parking garage. Pool towels were not stocked. Hotel staff were very inconvenienced when asked for help for services they were suppose to offer.

reviews.title: They overcharged and did not respond to my dispute
reviews.text: I noticed that the total charge was higher than what was quoted on Agoda. I reached out to *** but did not get a response after a week.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.negatives: The staff are a little bit to rude

reviews.title: Staff are rude
reviews.text: When we arrived, it was late at night with children. There was no other customers in the lobby but us. Staff didn’t want to serve us. In fact, when I walked up to be served they asked me to go to the next person and this happened twice. I asked if there were any rooms available and if they were busy and they said yes. I already had a room reserved but I wanted another one. When I looked online there was availability and then the staff acknowledged there were vacancies. Level of service was unacceptable. I also felt there was racism. We are from New Zealand and fair enough we are brown skinned but isn’t money, money! We had money to spend but the staff were rude so we stuck to sharing one room and couldn’t wait to leave. I would find somewhere else next time I take the kids to Disneyland. There are better hotels that are closer to the theme park. Skip the Hilton.

reviews.title: Good rest stop for Disneyland, poor communication with lobby .
reviews.positives: Property was clean and close to Disneyland.
reviews.negatives: I messaged the property 3 days prior asking for the two bedrooms I bought to be separate and not conjoining . They realtors saying they could accommodate . I checked in to the hotel and asked them again for separate rooms and triple checked to which the lady helping me said yes they are separate. Once I arrived at my room what do you know . Both rooms were conjoining . I called the hotel lobby to Inform them that again I was promised they would be seperate .  The male on the phone looked for about 15mins then said he’d call back . I ever received a call back and was stuck with conjoining rooms. Needless to say my message and speaking to them in person obviously didn’t matter to them and went in one ear and out another

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: No complimentary drinking water. Toiletries not for free. No bedroom slippers and ironing needs in the room

reviews.title: Great location but very disappointed that we were put in rooms with no AC
reviews.positives: Location
reviews.negatives: AC in the area of our rooms was not working during out entire stay and we found out that even days before we checked in they had issues with the AC not working in those same rooms.

reviews.title: Beautiful  service
reviews.text: Walking distance to Disney and restaurants.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: The hotel was nice
reviews.negatives: They charged for parking!.. 32 dollars for my vehicle plus tax!.. plus 58.00 dollars with tax for my friends vehicle.. Freaking ridiculous!!.. they charged my debit card a total of 95.94 just for parking!!.. your site said 30 plus 40 for an additional vehicle!.. I will never ever stay at a Hilton anything ever again!!.. I am pissed

reviews.title: Meh
reviews.text: This hotel is alright. Not a fan of the mandatory valet. They also take a deposit from you for said service. You do get it back but I feel some type of way about it. You would have to call the front desk if you want house keeping to come into your room. When I ws there I requested they come in twice, but never came. For the price that I paid here, I feel scammed since I have gotten way better service at other hotels in the area for less. Side note, this was during labor day weekend, so I did take that I to account.

reviews.title: The hotel was ok. I will not go back.
reviews.positives: Hotel common areas clean, Room fairly clean, Beds comfortable, and Valet parking.
reviews.negatives: Upon check in I was given keys to a room that was already occupied by a family. I unexpectedly walked in on them in their pajamas in bed. Scared us both! Front Desk apologized and gave us a different room. No discount for the inconvenience, no free parking, no free breakfast offerings or free water bottles. It almost felt like they thought it was funny. The next day they called and texted multiple times stating it was urgent for me to report to the front desk to put a credit card on file for incidentals as they forgot to enter it when I checked in the night before. Ridiculous! Hotel common areas clean, room clean, beds comfortable but carpet smelled like urine, valet parking nice, water pressure in shower terrible.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text: The new hotel is clean. The *** building and the *** building are connected, sharing facilities. Perhaps because there are not many rooms, there was no stress with the elevators. There were times when housekeeping did not come. When I checked, I was told they were supposed to come every day. There were several outlets and USB ports, but some of them did not work. The microwave was convenient. The Keurig-type coffee maker had coffee, decaf, Darjeeling, jasmine, and hot chocolate available, and I could make hot water without inserting a capsule. The provided cups were made of paper, so I thought they couldn't be used in the microwave. I had bought a heat-resistant measuring cup at the supermarket, so I used that to boil water in the microwave. It takes about a 10-minute walk to the entrance gate along the harbor to the park. It takes less than 5 minutes to get to security, and depending on the day and time, it can take some time for the security check. Shortly after leaving the hotel, I could see the monorail and the park, which made it feel close psychologically. I had no concerns about safety. I bought a salad and soup at Panera along the way and ate it in my room; it was delicious. You can pass through the parking lot of *** and ***. I had desserts at ***, which were American-sized and a fun experience. The room on the freeway side did have some noise, but I was tired, so it didn't prevent me from sleeping. The shower was not adjustable, and due to the low water flow, I found it somewhat difficult to use at times. Body soap, shampoo, and conditioner were ***. There is a laundry on the second floor. Using a credit card, it was $4 for each washer and dryer. The store sold cup noodles, snacks, sandwiches, and drinks, but I did not use it. Starbucks is open from the morning until 2 PM, but since I had breakfast at Luke & Laura, I did not utilize it. The breakfast area for guests staying at Home2 Suites was on the second floor. On the first day, I went to Anaheim Plaza with ***, gathered water and other supplies from ***, and had dinner at ***. Breakfast included fruit but no vegetables. There were egg dishes, bacon, sausage, yogurt, cereal, oatmeal, bread, and bagels, etc. I also had dinner at a bar that opens in the afternoon called *** before going to the park. The salmon, pizza-like bread, and buffalo chicken were very delicious, and I was able to enjoy a leisurely meal. It was a high-rate season, but I was lucky to find a relatively cheap plan by chance on Agoda.

reviews.title: Comfortable accommodations but price for stay is not commensurate.
reviews.positives: Location is close to disneyland
reviews.negatives: No designated smoking area. The staff at the restaurant were not accommodating but the staff at the front desk were very helpful and nice. Also unlike other hotels no slippers and bathrobe were provided. For the price of the accommodations, the absence if such simple things as slippers and robe is disappointing.

reviews.title: It's a bit of a walk to Disney.
reviews.text: The distance to Disney is acceptable! It was a hot day, so it was helpful to be able to return once. Since I don't understand much English, they explained using translation tools. Thank you.

reviews.title: I won’t stay here again, I will go to other hotels near Disneyland like I have before .
reviews.positives: convenient to Disneyland, the staff was great
reviews.negatives: we stayed 2 nights, we face to ask for them to go clean the room. other hotels they automatically do beds, clean room unless you don’t want to. all the hidden fees one has to pay after , like  parking!

reviews.title: Great for visiting Disneyland and very clean.
reviews.text: It is a 10-minute walk to Disneyland, and the walking distance is clean and pleasant. The interior is spacious, and the bed and bedding are clean and comfortable. It is convenient to have a microwave and a small refrigerator. However, the showerhead is attached to the ceiling, which makes washing inconvenient. Water is charged, and you must valet park your car, which costs $30 per day. However, for three days, there seems to be a lack of convenience stores or restaurants nearby, which might be the same even if you stay at other hotels. The building is newly constructed and clean. If I stay in Anaheim again, I would consider choosing this place.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.negatives: Beds were full size not queen

reviews.title: Beautiful new (?) Hotel
reviews.text:
So the first thing we experienced when arriving at the hotel is the $30 valet-only parking fee. Since there are no other parking options in the area, this is pretty much a hidden fee of the hotel. It’s pretty steep in that it wasn’t disclosed prior so I didn’t factor it in to a budget. Most other hotels we stay at offer free parking.

Beyond that, the second impression as the lobby. It’s spacious and has the ‘white tea’ sent off the Westin hotel - one of our favorites. That scent alone really calms us when we arrived.

Staff have been great! Unfortunately no free breakfast but you can pay for it if you want.

Rooms are very nice! Spacious, very clean, and lots of chargers/plugs for all our gadgets. The refridgerator in the room and coffee machine with pods are appreciated, as is the accessibility to an ice maker down the hall to use for water bottles on the hot days at Disneyland.

Overall, a great hotel that we’d come back to.

reviews.title: Walking distance to Disneyland. Good place
reviews.positives: Near Disneyland. Clean and polite staff.
reviews.negatives:
Broken USB outlets not working and not fixed during stay after notification of staff. 

The buffet breakfast could have been better.

reviews.title: Good!
reviews.text: Clean and not to far from disneyland

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.positives: We chose this hotel for the Hilton brand, we know what we get for the most part and are typically overly impressed with our stay so we don’t mind spending a little more for this. I can say the gentleman who checked us in was great, he accommodated us last minute to ensure we were able to have an early check in so that we could shower after 24 hours of flights and a quick trip to Disneyland after a delay in our flight. The beds were comfortable, the room was clean, and the breakfast was delicious and it was a short walk to Disneyland which was exactly what we wanted.
reviews.negatives: I was a little disappointed to discover that they did not carry the Crabtree and Evelyn products as most Hilton brands, as it is my absolute favorite. I was also very disappointed that even though it appeared as breakfast was included in the price we still had to go back and request our vouchers that were not given to us upon check in and it appears it was another extra charge when it shouldn’t have been. Also, the night staff was a little disappointing, again being exhausted from a very long day, we requested for some utensils to be brought up to our room and were basically told to go get them ourselves, mind you it was not a short walk to the desk and it is very out of our nature to even ask to begin with. I don’t feel as though it met our Hilton standard, and I’m not sure if it was Hilton or booking.com for the “extra” expenses but I’m not sure that I will be using this platform again to schedule until I’m able to dig a bit more into it.

reviews.title: Nice new hotel

reviews.title: Spacious rooms close to Disney
reviews.text: Large room with plenty of room for a couple. I got the deluxe king size bed room but arrived to a two queen bed room instead. No big deal. Pillows were very soft so keep in mind if you need more neck support. We also seemed to have crickets in the room we couldn’t locate. Nice breakfast at the downstairs Luke and Laura(sp?) I enjoyed the service. OverAll I would recommend this hotel if you are staying near Disneyland and want lots of room.

reviews.title: Amazing location and great homebase for a Disney vacation. We would stay there again.
reviews.positives: The location was perfect to access the Disney parks. It was about a 10-15 minute walk with a toddler. You can hear the highway if you listen for it in the middle of the night, but it was not loud and did not bother us.  The room was very big (for 2 adults and a toddler), clean and comfortable.  Housekeeping was really helpful and friendly.  The grab and go market was nice to have for last-minute food/drink needs and room service food was great. There were enough elevators and they were big enough that it was never an issue to catch one.
reviews.negatives: Our room had a connecting door to another room and our neighbors had a lot of people in the room and were very loud one evening/night, but otherwise it was not an issue. Valet parking seemed quite expensive, but it is probably similar at the other hotels in the area. The room window was partly covered due to the low floor level and overlooked a parking lot. The view was not important to us, but could be a consideration when booking and checking-in to confirm the room type and request a higher floor.

reviews.title: This hotel was amazing during my sorority's international conference. If I need to stay in Anaheim again I will defi
reviews.positives: I was impressed with how clean the property was overall. One of the staff members offered to get me coffee during the day. Although I didn't have a chance to eat, I appreciated the food options displayed on the marquee in the lobby.
reviews.negatives: I wasn’t a fan of having to valet park my car every day. I noticed that parking was quite scarce, and it was also a bit difficult to drive my rental car into the hotel entrance.

reviews.title: Surprisingly impressive, a great deal with kind workers.

reviews.title: Excellent, friendly staff, beautiful lobby, short walk to Disneyland.
